lesson_meta.title: A Childhood in Malabar: A Memoir
lesson_meta.subject: English

sections.title: 1. பாடச் சுருக்கம் (Full Summary of the Lesson)

content.text: This story is an excerpt from Kamala Das's memoir, describing a terrifying storm/cyclone that hit her ancestral home in Malabar.
The story begins with the family noticing the sky darkening and the wind picking up. Ammamma, sensing a cyclone, gathers everyone in the middle room upstairs.
As the storm intensifies with a 'roar like a vast crowd', the family moves to the 'thekkini' (southern room) downstairs, which is considered to have the strongest ceiling.
The author describes the chaos: a coconut palm crashing down, the courtyard (nalukettu) flooding with ice-cold water, and the sound of trees falling.
Characters like Meenakshi Edathi, though a distant relative, are depicted as indispensable to the family's daily happiness.
Ammayi (Kutti Oppu) arrives drenched and brave, despite the thunder and lightning. Even Thumbi, the pet dog, seeks shelter on the verandah.
The night is spent in fear and prayer. A servant woman wails in fear of never seeing her family again.
By morning, the rain stops. A young man named Balan arrives after wading through waist-high water to check on them, reporting the devastation of huts and trees in the village.
The story ends with the author feeling ashamed for asking about 'murukkus and dates' when others had lost so much in the storm.
Cultural Context: The story uses Malayalam kinship terms, giving us a glimpse into the traditional 'Nalukettu' houses of Kerala.
The Thekkini: The southern room of the house, usually used for storage or as a safe room during storms.
Nalukettu: A traditional style of architecture with a central courtyard and four wooden pillars.
The impact of nature: The story highlights the power of a cyclone and the human-animal bond (Thumbi the dog getting shelter).
Edathi - Brother's wife (அண்ணி)
Ettan - Elder brother (அண்ணன்)
Ammamma - Mother's mother (தாய் வழி பாட்டி)
Muthassi - Father's mother (தந்தை வழி பாட்டி)
Ammayi - Mother-in-law / Aunt (அத்தை)
Cheriamma - Mother's younger sister (சித்தி)
Kochu / Kutti Oppu - Nicknames used for younger sisters or cousins.
Glimmered - Shone with a faint light (மங்கலாக மின்னியது)
Slammed - Shut with force (வேகமாக சாத்தப்பட்டது)
Sob - Cry noisily (விம்மி அழுவது)
Collapse - Fall down suddenly (இடிந்து விழுதல்)
Make-shift - Temporary (தற்காலிகமான)
Drenched - Completely wet (முழுவதுமாக நனைந்தது)
Wail - A long high-pitched cry (புலம்பல்/அலறல்)
Casualties - People killed or injured (உயிரிழப்புகள்/சேதங்கள்)
Mundu - A traditional garment worn around the waist (வேட்டி போன்ற ஆடை)
The story took place in a village in Malabar, Kerala.
The storm started around 4 PM when it became pitch dark.
The family took shelter in the 'thekkini' because of its strong ceiling.
Sankaran Nair was the person who checked on the cows and the yard.
Thumbi was the pet dog from Ambazhathel family.
Balan from Vadekkara was the first to visit after the storm.
1. 'Why isn’t the snake coming?' - Meenakshi Edathi
2. 'I wonder whether it’s a cyclone.' - Ammamma
3. 'I want to see kutti oppu.' - Malathikutty
4. 'Will the house collapse?' - Muthassi
5. 'Is she crazy?' - Muthassi
1. Droplets of rain glimmered on Ammamma’s face. (True)
2. Malathikutty wanted to see kutti oppu. (True)
3. Ammayi comforted Malathikutty when she sobbed. (False - Ammamma comforted her initially)
4. The coconut palm crashed down due to heavy rain. (True)
1. Meenakshi Edathi was a _______ relative of the Ambazhathel family. (Answer: b. distant)
2. We suddenly heard the sound of the rain from the __________ like a roar. (Answer: b. southwest)
3. The ________ with four pillars began to overflow. (Answer: a. central hall)
4. __________ arrived drenched to the skin. (Answer: c. Ammayi)
5. There was knee-deep water in the ________. (Answer: a. courtyard)
1. Weather forecast is useful for farmers, fishermen, and navigators.
2. The climate of a place is permanent in nature.
3. India has a tropical monsoon climate.
4. Spring, Summer, autumn and winter are the four seasons.
5. Global warming is a change in the world's climate.
6. Satellite photographs help us predict tomorrow’s weather.
7. Spring is my favourite season.

questions.q: Who is the author of the lesson 'A Childhood in Malabar'? || Who is the author of the lesson 'A Childhood in Malabar'?
questions.ex: This lesson is an excerpt from the memoirs of the famous Indian writer Kamala Das. || This lesson is an excerpt from the memoirs of the famous Indian writer Kamala Das.

questions.q: In which direction did the family first hear the roar of the rain? || In which direction did the family first hear the roar of the rain?
questions.ex: The story mentions hearing the sound of rain from the south-west like the roar of a vast crowd. || The story mentions hearing the sound of rain from the south-west like the roar of a vast crowd.

questions.q: Where did Ammamma ask everyone to sit initially? || Where did Ammamma ask everyone to sit initially?
options: Middle room upstairs || Middle room upstairs
Southern room downstairs || Southern room downstairs
questions.ex: Initially, they gathered in the middle room upstairs and played with metal dice. || Initially, they gathered in the middle room upstairs and played with metal dice.

questions.q: What was the name of the pet dog that came to the verandah? || What was the name of the pet dog that came to the verandah?
questions.ex: Thumbi was the black-and-white pet dog from Ambazhathel who came in drenched. || Thumbi was the black-and-white pet dog from Ambazhathel who came in drenched.

questions.q: Why was the 'thekkini' chosen as the final shelter? || Why was the 'thekkini' chosen as the final shelter?
options: It was the biggest room || It was the biggest room
It was near the kitchen || It was near the kitchen
It had the strongest ceiling || It had the strongest ceiling
It was upstairs || It was upstairs
questions.ex: Ammaman’s mother instructed them to move there because it was the safest room. || Ammaman’s mother instructed them to move there because it was the safest room.

questions.q: What did the dry leaves do when the storm began? || What did the dry leaves do when the storm began?
options: They turned green || They turned green
They swirled upwards || They swirled upwards
They stayed still || They stayed still
They flew into the kitchen || They flew into the kitchen
questions.ex: The text says the dry leaves collected around the pond swirled upwards. || The text says the dry leaves collected around the pond swirled upwards.

questions.q: What does the word 'thekkini' mean in English? || What does the word 'thekkini' mean in English?
questions.ex: According to the glossary, thekkini refers to the southern room of the house. || According to the glossary, thekkini refers to the southern room of the house.

questions.q: What time did Ammamma say it was when it became 'pitch dark' outside? || What time did Ammamma say it was when it became 'pitch dark' outside?
options: Not even four || Not even four
questions.ex: Ammamma remarked that it wasn't even 4 PM but was already dark outside. || Ammamma remarked that it wasn't even 4 PM but was already dark outside.

questions.q: Who arrived at the house 'drenched to the skin' during the lightning? || Who arrived at the house 'drenched to the skin' during the lightning?
questions.ex: Ammayi arrived unmindful of the thunder and lightning, completely wet. || Ammayi arrived unmindful of the thunder and lightning, completely wet.

questions.q: What activity did Cheriamma suggest to help forget the fear? || What activity did Cheriamma suggest to help forget the fear?
options: Playing with dice || Playing with dice
Swimming in the courtyard || Swimming in the courtyard
questions.ex: Cheriamma suggested reciting verses (aksharaslokams) to distract everyone from the storm. || Cheriamma suggested reciting verses (aksharaslokams) to distract everyone from the storm.

questions.q: What was the 'nalukettu' mentioned in the story? || What was the 'nalukettu' mentioned in the story?
options: A type of boat || A type of boat
A central hall with four pillars || A central hall with four pillars
A heavy rainfall || A heavy rainfall
A traditional dish || A traditional dish
questions.ex: Nalukettu is a traditional Kerala house design with a central open courtyard. || Nalukettu is a traditional Kerala house design with a central open courtyard.

questions.q: Who was the servant woman's favorite god she called out to? || Who was the servant woman's favorite god she called out to?
questions.ex: In her panic, she wailed calling 'My Guruvayoorappa!'. || In her panic, she wailed calling 'My Guruvayoorappa!'.

questions.q: Analogy: Ammamma : Mother's mother :: Muthassi : ? || Analogy: Ammamma : Mother's mother :: Muthassi : ?
questions.ex: In Malayalam, Muthassi is used for the father's mother. || In Malayalam, Muthassi is used for the father's mother.

questions.q:
Assertion (A): The family moved to the southern room downstairs.
Reason (R): The southern room had the strongest ceiling to protect them. || Assertion (A): The family moved to the southern room downstairs.
Reason (R): The southern room had the strongest ceiling to protect them.
options: Both A and R are true, and R explains A || Both A and R are true, and R explains A
Both A and R are true, but R does not explain A || Both A and R are true, but R does not explain A
A is true, but R is false || A is true, but R is false
A is false, but R is true || A is false, but R is true
questions.ex: The reason why they moved to the thekkini was its structural strength during the cyclone. || The reason why they moved to the thekkini was its structural strength during the cyclone.

questions.q: What did Sankaran Nair check regarding the cowshed? || What did Sankaran Nair check regarding the cowshed?
options: If it was empty || If it was empty
If the beams were strong enough || If the beams were strong enough
If the cows were hungry || If the cows were hungry
If the gate was locked || If the gate was locked
questions.ex: He checked the cowshed and said the beams were strong, so it wouldn't fall. || He checked the cowshed and said the beams were strong, so it wouldn't fall.

questions.q: What was the color of the pet dog Thumbi? || What was the color of the pet dog Thumbi?
questions.ex: The text describes Thumbi as the black-and-white pet dog. || The text describes Thumbi as the black-and-white pet dog.

questions.q: How high was the water at the gate when Balan arrived in the morning? || How high was the water at the gate when Balan arrived in the morning?
options: Over the head || Over the head
questions.ex: A young man (Balan) stood smiling in waist-high water at the gate. || A young man (Balan) stood smiling in waist-high water at the gate.

questions.q: Why did the author hang her head in shame at the end? || Why did the author hang her head in shame at the end?
options: Because she broke a glass || Because she broke a glass
Because she asked for snacks despite the tragedy || Because she asked for snacks despite the tragedy
Because she didn't help Ammamma || Because she didn't help Ammamma
Because she was afraid of the rain || Because she was afraid of the rain
questions.ex: When Balan reported deaths of animals and destruction, the author asked for murukkus, making her feel ashamed. || When Balan reported deaths of animals and destruction, the author asked for murukkus, making her feel ashamed.

questions.q:
Match the characters with their actions:
1. Sankaran Nair - a. Arrived drenched
2. Ammayi - b. Checked the cowshed
3. Balan - c. Waded through water at dawn || Match the characters with their actions:
1. Sankaran Nair - a. Arrived drenched
2. Ammayi - b. Checked the cowshed
3. Balan - c. Waded through water at dawn
questions.ex: Sankaran checked the shed, Ammayi arrived wet, and Balan waded through the water. || Sankaran checked the shed, Ammayi arrived wet, and Balan waded through the water.

questions.q: Which plant is mentioned to have crashed down during the storm? || Which plant is mentioned to have crashed down during the storm?
questions.ex: They heard a coconut palm crashing down, which made Muthassi worry about the house. || They heard a coconut palm crashing down, which made Muthassi worry about the house.

questions.q: What is the meaning of the word 'mundu'? || What is the meaning of the word 'mundu'?
options: A type of food || A type of food
A traditional garment || A traditional garment
A Malayalam month || A Malayalam month
A heavy storm || A heavy storm
questions.ex: Mundu is the traditional white garment/dhoti worn in Kerala. || Mundu is the traditional white garment/dhoti worn in Kerala.

questions.q: Identify the incorrect pair based on the glossary: || Identify the incorrect pair based on the glossary:
options: Ettan - Elder brother || Ettan - Elder brother
Edathi - Elder sister || Edathi - Elder sister
Ammamma - Mother's mother || Ammamma - Mother's mother
questions.ex: According to the text glossary, Edathi means Brother's wife (Anni). || According to the text glossary, Edathi means Brother's wife (Anni).

questions.q: What kind of 'memorial' or 'record' does a fact file on energy resources create? || What kind of 'memorial' or 'record' does a fact file on energy resources create?
questions.ex: The project section G asks to make a fact file on energy resources. || The project section G asks to make a fact file on energy resources.

questions.ex: The climate of a place is generally permanent and stable compared to daily weather. || The climate of a place is generally permanent and stable compared to daily weather.

questions.q: What helps scientists predict tomorrow's weather? || What helps scientists predict tomorrow's weather?
questions.ex: Satellite photographs help us predict tomorrow’s weather. || Satellite photographs help us predict tomorrow’s weather.

questions.q: Situation: If you are in a house during a cyclone, what is the best thing to do according to the lesson? || Situation: If you are in a house during a cyclone, what is the best thing to do according to the lesson?
options: Run outside to watch trees fall || Run outside to watch trees fall
Sit in a room with a strong ceiling and stay quiet || Sit in a room with a strong ceiling and stay quiet
Swim in the flooded courtyard || Swim in the flooded courtyard
Stand near the windows to see Kutti Oppu || Stand near the windows to see Kutti Oppu
questions.ex: Ammamma advised everyone to sit quietly and pray in the safest part of the house. || Ammamma advised everyone to sit quietly and pray in the safest part of the house.

questions.q: What did the servant woman take refuge in? || What did the servant woman take refuge in?
options: A makeshift toilet adjacent to the room || A makeshift toilet adjacent to the room
The upstairs bedroom || The upstairs bedroom
questions.ex: The servant woman took refuge in the makeshift toilet adjacent to the thekkini. || The servant woman took refuge in the makeshift toilet adjacent to the thekkini.

questions.q: What does 'Kochu' refer to in the sentence 'Kochu, what was that?' || What does 'Kochu' refer to in the sentence 'Kochu, what was that?'
options: A small dog || A small dog
A type of bird || A type of bird
questions.ex: Muthassi calls Ammamma 'Kochu' in the story. || Muthassi calls Ammamma 'Kochu' in the story.

questions.q: Who was the 'important person' in the Ambazhathel family without whom they couldn't exist happily? || Who was the 'important person' in the Ambazhathel family without whom they couldn't exist happily?
questions.ex: The text states that without Meenakshi Edathi, the family could not have existed happily for a single day. || The text states that without Meenakshi Edathi, the family could not have existed happily for a single day.

questions.q: What are 'murukkus'? || What are 'murukkus'?
options: Traditional Malayali snacks || Traditional Malayali snacks
Heavy rain clouds || Heavy rain clouds
questions.ex: Murukkus are savory, crunchy snacks mentioned in the text. || Murukkus are savory, crunchy snacks mentioned in the text.

questions.q: What was the weather like when it was 'not even four'? || What was the weather like when it was 'not even four'?
options: Bright and sunny || Bright and sunny
questions.ex: Ammamma said it was pitch dark outside despite being early afternoon. || Ammamma said it was pitch dark outside despite being early afternoon.

questions.q: What sound scared Ammamma in the beginning? || What sound scared Ammamma in the beginning?
options: A dog barking || A dog barking
The sound of the cyclone/wind || The sound of the cyclone/wind
questions.ex: Ammamma said, 'The sound of it scares me,' referring to the impending storm. || Ammamma said, 'The sound of it scares me,' referring to the impending storm.

questions.q: Analogy: Rainy : Downpour :: Bright : ? || Analogy: Rainy : Downpour :: Bright : ?
questions.ex: Rainy weather features downpours; bright weather is sunny. || Rainy weather features downpours; bright weather is sunny.

questions.q: Who said 'The cowshed won’t fall down, Valiamma'? || Who said 'The cowshed won’t fall down, Valiamma'?
options: The servant woman || The servant woman
questions.ex: Sankaran Nair reassured Ammamma (Valiamma) about the strength of the cowshed. || Sankaran Nair reassured Ammamma (Valiamma) about the strength of the cowshed.

questions.q: What was floating in the water after the storm according to Balan? || What was floating in the water after the storm according to Balan?
options: Dead goats and fowls || Dead goats and fowls
questions.ex: Balan describes the tragic sight of dead poultry and floating goats in the floodwater. || Balan describes the tragic sight of dead poultry and floating goats in the floodwater.

questions.q: What did Sankaran spread on the verandah for the dog Thumbi? || What did Sankaran spread on the verandah for the dog Thumbi?
options: A soft blanket || A soft blanket
A gunny bag || A gunny bag
questions.ex: Sankaran spread a gunny bag for Thumbi to lie down and sleep on. || Sankaran spread a gunny bag for Thumbi to lie down and sleep on.

questions.q: Which of these is a 'Season' according to the lesson? || Which of these is a 'Season' according to the lesson?
questions.ex: Spring, Summer, Autumn, and Winter are specifically listed as the four seasons. || Spring, Summer, Autumn, and Winter are specifically listed as the four seasons.

questions.q: What did the family do to pass the time while sitting in the upstairs room? || What did the family do to pass the time while sitting in the upstairs room?
options: Played with metal dice || Played with metal dice
Cleaned the house || Cleaned the house
questions.ex: Ammamma gave them metal dice to play with to keep them occupied. || Ammamma gave them metal dice to play with to keep them occupied.

questions.q: In the evening, who was Malathikutty desperate to see? || In the evening, who was Malathikutty desperate to see?
questions.ex: Malathikutty kept crying, 'I want to see Kutti Oppu'. || Malathikutty kept crying, 'I want to see Kutti Oppu'.

questions.q: What happened to the water in the sunken courtyard of the nalukettu? || What happened to the water in the sunken courtyard of the nalukettu?
options: It dried up || It dried up
It began to overflow || It began to overflow
It turned into ice || It turned into ice
It was used for drinking || It was used for drinking
questions.ex: The water collected in the courtyard and began to overflow as the rain continued. || The water collected in the courtyard and began to overflow as the rain continued.

questions.q:
Assertion (A): The author hanging her head in shame is the conclusion of the story.
Reason (R): She realized her own selfishness in asking for snacks while the village suffered. || Assertion (A): The author hanging her head in shame is the conclusion of the story.
Reason (R): She realized her own selfishness in asking for snacks while the village suffered.
options: Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation || Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation
Both A and R are true, but R is not the correct explanation || Both A and R are true, but R is not the correct explanation
A is true, R is false || A is true, R is false
A is false, R is true || A is false, R is true
questions.ex: Her shame came from the contrast between her childish desire for treats and the real tragedy Balan described. || Her shame came from the contrast between her childish desire for treats and the real tragedy Balan described.

questions.q: What was the appearance of Meenakshi Edathi described in the jumbled sentences? || What was the appearance of Meenakshi Edathi described in the jumbled sentences?
options: Fair and young || Fair and young
Dark-skinned and middle-aged || Dark-skinned and middle-aged
Very tall and thin || Very tall and thin
Short with grey hair || Short with grey hair
questions.ex: The text describes her as a dark-skinned, middle-aged woman. || The text describes her as a dark-skinned, middle-aged woman.

questions.q: Identify the weather icon that represents 'Thunder and Lightning': || Identify the weather icon that represents 'Thunder and Lightning':
options: Cloud with rain icon || Cloud with rain icon
Cloud with a bolt icon || Cloud with a bolt icon
questions.ex: A bolt of electricity usually symbolizes thunder and lightning. || A bolt of electricity usually symbolizes thunder and lightning.

questions.q: What was the temperature of the water in the courtyard according to the narrator? || What was the temperature of the water in the courtyard according to the narrator?
questions.ex: The narrator grumbled that the water was 'ice-cold'. || The narrator grumbled that the water was 'ice-cold'.

questions.q: Who came from 'Vadekkara' to check on the family? || Who came from 'Vadekkara' to check on the family?
questions.ex: Balan arrived in the morning, saying he was from Vadekkara. || Balan arrived in the morning, saying he was from Vadekkara.

questions.q: Why was the servant woman hitting her head with her hands? || Why was the servant woman hitting her head with her hands?
options: She had a headache || She had a headache
She was in extreme panic and fear || She was in extreme panic and fear
She was trying to fix her hair || She was trying to fix her hair
She was playing a game || She was playing a game
questions.ex: In her desperation and fear of dying, she wailed and hit her head. || In her desperation and fear of dying, she wailed and hit her head.

questions.q: What is 'Global Warming' related to in the 'Steps to Success' section? || What is 'Global Warming' related to in the 'Steps to Success' section?
options: Change in season || Change in season
Change in world's climate || Change in world's climate
questions.ex: Global warming is defined as a change in the world's climate. || Global warming is defined as a change in the world's climate.

questions.q: Which tree's seat of the swing fell down? || Which tree's seat of the swing fell down?
questions.ex: The jumbled sentences mention that the seat of the swing fell down from the ilanji tree. || The jumbled sentences mention that the seat of the swing fell down from the ilanji tree.

questions.q: How did Balan travel to the house in the morning? || How did Balan travel to the house in the morning?
options: Waded through water || Waded through water
questions.ex: Balan started at daybreak and waded through the floodwater. || Balan started at daybreak and waded through the floodwater.

questions.q: What was the overall atmosphere of the story during the night? || What was the overall atmosphere of the story during the night?
options: Fear and uncertainty || Fear and uncertainty
questions.ex: The storm, falling trees, and wailing servant created an atmosphere of fear and survival. || The storm, falling trees, and wailing servant created an atmosphere of fear and survival.
